content: Sebastien forwarded intro to Sarah Chen via Sequoia operator network. Former CMO at Figma, 8yr tenure, led positioning through Series B→IPO. Available immediately post-vesting cliff. Asking $240K base + equity package TBD.
summary: Sarah Chen intro from Sequoia — ex-Figma CMO, available now

content: Marketing Director hire for Series A is blocking top-of-funnel momentum. Acme, Initech, Globex all in pilot stage but no dedicated inbound strategy. Need someone who can build repeatable demand gen while we close seed follow-on. Role should own positioning, analyst relations, content.
summary: Marketing Director critical for Series A readiness and pipeline

content: Phone screen with Sarah Chen, 45min. Asked about our Anthropic/Voyage AI integrations, competitive moat vs. pinecone/llamaindex. Pushed back on TAM sizing—wanted to see bottom-up logos, not top-down. Impressed by rigor. Said yes to full loop.
summary: Sarah Chen phone screen — sharp on product, wants evidence-based TAM

content: Anya flagged: if we hire Sarah at $240K, that's 3.2% of current burn ($750K/mo ops spend). Lina and Ari both want to sit in interviews. Scheduled panel for Apr 17, 2pm PT. Added Lina, Ari, Anya. Sebastien will close.
summary: Sarah Chen full loop scheduled Apr 17 — ops impact flagged

content: Priya sent comp data: B2B SaaS CMOs at $200–280K range depending on stage/geography. Sarah's ask at 75th percentile. Recommended we counter at $210K base + 0.5% equity (refresher vesting 4yr/1yr cliff). Noted her Figma reputation is substantial—risk is overpay, not underpay.
summary: Comp benchmark: Sarah at 75th %ile, Priya suggests $210K counter

content: Sarah's interview panel feedback: Lina loved her Figma positioning playbook for dev tools, asked if she'd adapt it. Ari concerned about her lack of hands-on AI/ML depth—worried she'd over-delegate to agency. Anya noted strong financial discipline. Sebastien said 'hire her if comp works.'
summary: Panel split: Lina/Anya/Sebastien yes; Ari wants more AI chops

content: Process for next marketing hire: (1) Source via operator networks first (Sequoia, a16z, YC) before search firm. (2) Phone screen must probe product depth & TAM methodology. (3) Panel includes Lina (product fit), Ari (tech credibility), Anya (financial planning). (4) Close conversation owned by Sebastien.
summary: Documented: marketing hire sourcing & interview workflow for future

content: Sarah countered our $210K offer with $225K + 0.55% equity. Anya green-lit. Offer sent. Contingent on background check, reference calls with her Figma skip-level (got intro from her manager). Start date: May 13, 2026 (post-vesting cliff settlement).
summary: Sarah accepts $225K/$55K equity, starts May 13 post-vesting

content: Reference call with Sarah's Figma skip (Chief Product, ex-Stripe). Said she was 'exceptional at positioning complex infra to design orgs, brutal with underperforming vendors (cut $2M MarTech stack), hired only A-players.' No red flags.
summary: Reference: exceptional positioning, vendor discipline, high hiring bar

content: Key assumption we're making with Sarah: she can translate SaaS developer-tools playbook to AI agents space. Positioning will emphasize Clude's edge (Anthropic/Voyage integration, sub-500ms latency) vs. open-source competitors. Need to validate TAM sizing before Series A pitch.
summary: Sarah's mandate: apply Figma dev-tools playbook to agent market

content: Slack thread with Sebastien: discussed Sarah's first 90 days. Priorities: (1) position for Series A (Sequoia, Benchmark, GGV). (2) Rebuild analyst relations (Gartner, Forrester gaps). (3) Customer case studies (Acme Corp + Wayne Industries closest to done). (4) Content calendar for DevRel.
summary: Sarah's 90-day roadmap: Series A positioning, analyst relations, case studies

content: Onboarding doc created in Notion. Links to: Clude product roadmap (Linear), customer list (HubSpot), AWS/Cloudflare/Sentry dashboards, DataDog metrics, GitHub repos. Set Sarah up with 1Password vault. First day: product deep-dive with Ari, then customer calls with Marcus.
summary: Sarah's onboarding prepped: infra access, vendor logins, first-day schedule

content: I noticed I default to async docs + structured due diligence (comp benchmarks, reference calls, structured panel feedback). Makes hiring faster and avoids re-litigating decisions. With Sarah, felt useful—everyone was aligned by offer stage. Will use this template for future hires.
summary: I prefer async hiring rigor—avoids re-explaining, speeds decisions

content: Lessons from Sarah hire: (1) Operator networks are faster/better than search firms for early-stage SaaS. (2) Panel format with clear evaluation criteria prevents groupthink. (3) Comp at 75th %ile was right—she countered minimally. (4) Reference calls worth the time; skip-level validates cultural fit.
summary: Hiring learnings: networks beat search firms, panel rigor works, pay fairly
